metadata.title: Intent Layer: The Five Questions Claude Cannot Answer for You

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Ciao, Liam — in for Bear. Generating the intent is the work? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Generating the intent is the work?
props.segment: Intent Layer: The Five Questions Claude

beats.narration_text: He asked Claude to generate a PROJECT.md for his data visualization project. Claude returned a professional document: goals, stakeholders, success metrics, timeline. He pasted it into his project. His instructor read it. She asked: could this be anyone's data visualization project? He reread it. Yes. It could. Nothing in it was specific to his choices, his audience, his definition of done. He had asked Claude to generate the intent. That was the mistake. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, generate PROJECT.md for data visualization project

props.command: generate a PROJECT.md for my data visualization project

beats.narration_text: The U.S. Copyright Office's September 2023 guidance on AI-generated work introduced a test: expressive choices made by the human author. The Théâtre D'Opéra Spatial ruling — de minimis — found that 80 hours of AI iteration did not constitute authorship because the human choices were not sufficiently traceable in the output. A generated PROJECT.md passes no test. It contains no choices. It contains plausible structure that could describe any project.
beats.new_visual_element: Received not authored — Copyright Office 2023 / de minimis / 80h iteration ≠ authorship / plausible ≠ chosen

beats.narration_text: The Intent Layer is five questions answered in the human's voice before Claude sees anything. Who is this for, specifically — not 'stakeholders.' What should they feel when they use it — not 'informed.' What does this explicitly refuse to do? What does done look like — the checkable test, not 'successful completion.' What is out of scope? Claude can copy-edit the answers. Claude cannot generate them. Generating the intent is the work.
beats.new_visual_element: Five questions — who / feel / refuse / done / out of scope / human voice / Claude copy-edits not generates

beats.narration_text: The five questions are non-delegable. If you cannot answer 'who is this for, specifically,' you do not yet know who it is for. If you cannot answer 'what does this refuse,' you have not decided what the scope boundary is. The refusal is the hard part — it is easier to add features than to commit to exclusions. The Intent Layer forces the exclusion decision in writing, before any code runs. If you cannot answer, that is the signal. The project is not ready to build.
beats.new_visual_element: Non-delegable — cannot answer = project not ready / refusal is hardest / exclusions in writing / before code runs
beats.spark_line: Cannot answer? Not ready to build.

beats.narration_text: The Intent Layer is the document that proves a person decided what the work is for. It is not a formality. It is the answer to: which of these decisions did you make? If every answer can be found in the PROJECT.md — in your words, before Claude generated anything — the work is yours in the way that matters. If Claude generated it, you received it. Those are different outcomes, and the difference is visible.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — Intent Layer in your voice / five answers traceable / Claude copy-edits after / proof of authorship

beats.narration_text: Answer the five questions before your next build starts. Paste this.
beats.new_visual_element: HANDOFF — Intent Layer five questions prompt

props.command: I am going to answer five questions about this project before you generate anything. These are my answers, not prompts for you to answer: (1) Who is this for, specifically — [your answer], (2) What should they feel when they use it — [your answer], (3) What does this explicitly refuse to do — [your answer], (4) What does done look like, the checkable test — [your answer], (5) What is out of scope — [your answer]. Once I paste my answers, copy-edit them for clarity only. Do not add features, expand scope, or generate intent I have not stated.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. The Intent Layer is the document that proves a person decided what the work is for. It is not a formality. It is the answer to: which of these decisions did you make? If every answer can be found in the PROJECT.md — in your words, before Claude generated anything — the work is yours in the way that matters. If Claude generated it, you received it. Those are different outcomes, and the difference is visible. Answer the five questions before your next build starts. Paste this.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Intent Layer: The Five Questions Claude Cannot Answer for You] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
